todaysEvents.summary: Iran FM Araghchi visits Oman Sultan Haitham after Islamabad round - traditional US-Iran back-channel; returns to Islamabad (Apr 26); next stop Moscow. Araghchi: deal 'just inches away' but criticizes 'maximalist US demands'. Round 2 paused not collapsed - Oman channel re-opened (ANI, Washington Times, CNN, Apr 26).
todaysEvents.delta: De-escalation signal: Oman back-channel reopens after Islamabad failure; shuttle diplomacy continues; tail risk revised to 47% from 50%

todaysEvents.summary: Trump unharmed in third assassination attempt at WH Correspondents' Dinner (Apr 25 Sat, Washington Hilton); suspect Cole Allen (31, CA) in custody after exchanging fire with Secret Service - one agent hit, protected by vest; Trump, Vance, Cabinet safe. Iran mocks Trump in video response (Al Jazeera, Apr 26); no Iran nexus confirmed (CNN, Fox News, Al Jazeera, Apr 25-26).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Domestic US event; no Iran connection; adds political risk premium to oil forecast ($103-112 band Apr 27 est.)

todaysEvents.summary: Ceasefire nominally active; Hormuz US blockade and IRGC enforcement both intact; Iran holds 3 Western vessels (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas, Euphoria); no new kinetic events; mine-clearing ~6 months per Pentagon; Brent ~$106 est. Monday open; tail risk 47%.
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral/Lateral: No change in physical Hormuz access; diplomatic picture modestly improved vs D55 on Oman back-channel; structural disruption unchanged

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Oman back-channel yields breakthrough - Araghchi-Sultan Haitham meeting (Apr 26) reopens indirect US-Iran channel after Islamabad failure. Iran accepts modified framework; vessels returned; ceasefire formally extended. Narrow (9%) - US still labels Iranian demands maximalist; Araghchi heading to Moscow next; mine-clearing ~6 months regardless of deal (PBS, Fox News); full commercial reopening delayed. Brent retreats toward $92-98/bbl on deal; iTraxx tightens toward 180bp.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Oman shuttle stalemate - Araghchi continues regional tour (Oman to Islamabad to Moscow) without breakthrough; US blockade and IRGC Hormuz enforcement co-exist; selective bilateral transit; periodic vessel seizures. Brent stabilises $95-115/bbl; JKM $17-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 200-220bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (44%) - shuttle diplomacy active but no deal in reach.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Shuttle diplomacy fails - Araghchi Moscow visit yields no breakthrough; US 'shoot and kill' ROE triggers incident with IRGC small boat; ceasefire formally terminated. Gulf-wide blockade activated; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis. Tail at 47% (down from 50%) - Oman back-channel tentatively active but 3 vessels held, blockade intact, mine-clearing 6 months unchanged.
